# 30 — Slot Rebuild: Slack Analysis (R-1 Step 3)

**Status:** research findings; informs Stage 2 of Invasion Regions feature.
**Generated by:** `backend/core/slot_slack_test.go::TestSlotSlackAnalysis`
**Date:** 2026-04-26

## Goal

Quantify how much **trailing zero slack** exists between the
`unlocked_regions` block and the end of each save slot. The hybrid blob
rebuild (`backend/core/slot_rebuild.go`) absorbs delta from a mutated
`UnlockedRegions` slice by trimming or zero-padding the
`post_unlocked_regions` blob. If the trailing bytes of that blob are
non-zero, trimming corrupts the save.

`SlotSize = 0x280000`, `DlcSectionOffset = 0x27FF4E`, `HashOffset = 0x27FF80`.

## Raw measurements

| save                | slot | version | regions | regs_end   | post_blob_sz | trail_zero | extra_fits | last_nz_pre_DLC | last_nz_pre_hash | last_nz_all |
|---------------------|------|---------|---------|------------|--------------|------------|------------|-----------------|------------------|-------------|
| oisis_pl-org.txt    | 0    | 230     | 354     | 0x1EE8C    | 2,494,658    | 0          | 0          | 0x27FF4D        | 0x27FF7F         | 0x27FFFF    |
| oisis_pl-org.txt    | 1    | 251     | 6       | 0x19A51    | 2,516,221    | 0          | 0          | 0x27FF4D        | 0x27FF7E         | 0x27FFFF    |
| oisisk_ps4.txt      | 0    | 150     | 4       | 0x19891    | 2,516,669    | 0          | 0          | 0x27FF4D        | 0x27FF7D         | 0x27FFF9    |
| oisisk_ps4.txt      | 1    | 150     | 4       | 0x19889    | 2,516,677    | 1          | 0          | 0x27FF4C        | 0x27FF7C         | 0x27FFFC    |
| oisisk_ps4.txt      | 2    | 150     | 4       | 0x19889    | 2,516,677    | 41         | 10         | 0x27FF24        | 0x27FF24         | 0x27FFE4    |
| oisisk_ps4.txt      | 3    | 150     | 6       | 0x19A49    | 2,516,229    | 1          | 0          | 0x27FF4C        | 0x27FF7F         | 0x27FFFC    |
| ER0000.sl2 (PC)     | 0    | 250     | 319     | 0x1C2B3    | 2,505,883    | 0          | 0          | 0x27FF4D        | 0x27FF7D         | 0x27FFF9    |
| ER0000.sl2 (PC)     | 1    | 251     | 395     | 0x1C328    | 2,505,766    | 102        | 25         | 0x27FEE7        | 0x27FF7F         | 0x27FFF3    |
| ER0000.sl2 (PC)     | 2    | 230     | 29      | 0x19D50    | 2,515,454    | 6          | 1          | 0x27FF47        | 0x27FF7C         | 0x27FFFB    |
| ER0000.sl2 (PC)     | 3    | 250     | 110     | 0x1A97A    | 2,512,340    | 164        | 41         | 0x27FEA9        | 0x27FEA9         | 0x27FFEB    |
| ER0000.sl2 (PC)     | 4    | 251     | 6       | 0x199F3    | 2,516,315    | 9          | 2          | 0x27FF44        | 0x27FF7C         | 0x27FFF6    |

`extra_fits = trailing_zeros / 4` — each region ID is 4 bytes.

## Findings

1. **Slack is wildly variable** — between 0 and 164 trailing zero bytes
   in the `post_unlocked_regions` blob. Five slots out of eleven have
   **zero usable slack**: any growth of `unlocked_regions` overwrites
   live bytes.

2. **`last_nz_pre_DLC` is almost always 0x27FF4D**, i.e. one byte before
   `DlcSectionOffset`. The `post_unlocked_regions` blob runs flush
   against the DLC section in the majority of saves — the
   "shift-everything-by-N-bytes" approach we tried in Stage 2 had
   nowhere to absorb the shift.

3. **DLC section bytes [3..49] are not all zero in our saves** —
   `last_nz_pre_hash` lands in the DLC range (0x27FF4E..0x27FF7F) for
   8 of 11 slots, contradicting the older assumption that "DLC bytes
   [3..49] must be 0x00". `oisisk_ps4.txt slot 2` is the exception
   (entire DLC section zero), suggesting DLC content is *populated as
   the player progresses*, not statically zero.

4. **Maximum slack across the tested fleet is 41 extra regions**
   (`ER0000.sl2 slot 3`, 164 trailing zeros). Stage 2 needs to be able
   to add up to 78 regions ("Unlock All"). For 5 of 11 slots, **even
   one extra region cannot fit**. Hybrid trim is therefore insufficient
   for the user-facing feature.

## Implications for R-1

The hybrid blob approach (current implementation in
`backend/core/slot_rebuild.go`) **cannot deliver Stage 2 as specified**.
It will succeed for `ER0000.sl2 slot 3` (41 extra regions), partially
work for a few other PC slots, and fail outright on all PS4 slots.

Two viable paths forward:

### Option A — Conservative Stage 2 (pragmatic)

Keep the hybrid rebuild. At write time, compute the effective slack
(`trailing_zeros / 4`) and reject mutations that would exceed it with a
clear error: "save has space for N more regions; please remove some
first." The frontend would gate "Unlock All" behind a per-slot capacity
check.

- **Pros:** ships in days, no further parser work.
- **Cons:** "Unlock All" effectively never works for fresh / heavy saves
  (5/11 slots can't add even one region). Confusing UX.

### Option B — Full struct rebuild (matches er-save-manager)

Parse every section after `unlocked_regions` (horse, blood_stain,
menu_profile_save_load, gaitem_game_data, tutorial_data, event_flags,
field_area, world_area, world_geom_man, world_geom_man2, rend_man,
player_coordinates, net_man, weather, time, base_version, steam_id,
ps5_activity, dlc, hash) into typed structures. On rebuild, serialize
each at its **original size** in order; the slot ends with a tail
padding of zeros that absorbs any `unlocked_regions` delta.

- **Pros:** robust; matches the proven Python reference; supports any
  reasonable region count.
- **Cons:** ~30 mini-parsers + serializers; the size-prefixed sections
  (`field_area`, `world_area`, `world_geom_man`, `world_geom_man2`,
  `rend_man`) have undocumented internal layouts on our side — they need
  to be treated as `(size: u32, data: bytes)` opaque blobs at minimum.
  Estimated effort 15–25 h.

### Option C — Hybrid with anchored-tail relaxation

Mid-ground: keep current section model, but **stop pinning DLC and hash
to the end of the slot**. Instead, place the post-regions blob, then
DLC, then hash, then zero-pad to `SlotSize`. This relies on the
hypothesis that DLC and hash positions are *not* fixed offsets but
rather "wherever the previous sections end". Verifying this requires
hand-crafting a save with shifted DLC offset and confirming the game
loads it.

- **Pros:** small code delta (~50 LOC change in `RebuildSlot`), no new
  parsers.
- **Cons:** unverified hypothesis; if wrong, save corrupts the same way
  Stage 2 did. **Single Steam Deck test would falsify or confirm it.**

## Recommendation

**Test Option C first** — it costs one day and one Steam Deck round-trip.
If the game accepts a save with DLC at a non-canonical offset, we can
ship Stage 2 with minimal additional code. If it rejects the save, fall
back to Option B (full struct rebuild) and budget accordingly.

Tracking decision in `docs/ROADMAP.md` under
"Invasion Regions Toggle" → Stage 2 entry.

---

## Update 2026-04-26 — original measurements were misleading

After implementing the full sequential parser (R-1 Steps 4–13), the real
slack picture turned out to be much more favourable than this section
suggested. The original measurement looked at the gap between
`unlocked_regions_end` and the **fixed** `DlcSectionOffset = 0x27FF4E`.
That offset is a *convention* — every save we have happens to place DLC
at exactly `SlotSize - 0xB2`, but it is not a pinned game-side offset.

The full parser reaches end-of-data around byte ~2.2 MB for every active
slot (PS4 + PC), leaving **408KB–432KB of zero tail padding** before the
end of the 0x280000-byte slot. DLC is just the second-to-last fixed-size
section *inside* `TrailingFixedBlock`; what looked like a "pinned" DLC
position is simply where the section ends up on a typical save.

| save             | slot | parsed (pos) | tail rest |
|------------------|------|--------------|-----------|
| oisis_pl-org PS4 | 0    | 2,212,520    | 408,920   |
| oisis_pl-org PS4 | 1    | 2,189,717    | 431,723   |
| ER0000.sl2 PC    | 0    | 2,202,359    | 419,081   |
| ER0000.sl2 PC    | 1    | 2,204,780    | 416,660   |
| ER0000.sl2 PC    | 2    | 2,189,228    | 432,212   |
| ER0000.sl2 PC    | 3    | 2,196,222    | 425,218   |
| ER0000.sl2 PC    | 4    | 2,188,767    | 432,673   |

**Implication:** `RebuildSlot` (Step 13) followed by
`SetUnlockedRegions` (Step 14) supports adding ~100,000 regions on every
slot tested — Stage 2 is unconstrained for realistic input (≤ 78 regions).
The Steam Deck test in Step 15 still gates the path, but the "Option B
costs 15–25 h" budget paid for itself: now we have full sequential
parsers for every section, ready for future weather/teleport/etc.
features.
